description: This chart plots specific hardness (unit: HV·cm³/g) against density (unit: g/cm³) to compare the performance of developed Fe-Mn-Al-Cu low-density steels (15Al, 17Al, 19Al series, with Al atomic content increasing from 15% to 19%) with reference materials including other low-density steels, miscellaneous steels (stainless steel, high-speed steel, ferritic/martensitic steel, carbon steel, etc.), pure iron, and medium/high entropy alloys (M/HEAs). The developed steels show densities in the range of 6.66 to 6.85 g/cm³, with a maximum specific hardness exceeding 140 HV·cm³/g, which is significantly higher than the ~120 HV·cm³/g upper limit of the compared reference materials, and higher Al content corresponds to lower alloy density. This figure supports the core conclusion that aging-treated Fe-Mn-Al-Cu low-density steels realize ultra-high specific hardness, outperforming most existing structural metallic materials in this performance index.
